Subscription state machine transitions into canceled (terminal)

action.name: markContactChurned
action.description: Mark crm.contacts.lifecycle_stage=churned + log a churn activity. Resolves contact via subscription.deal_source_ref → crm.deals.contact_id, falling back to customers.contact_source_ref.

trigger.description: Subscription transition that changes the tenant MRR (new-sub / churn / upgrade / downgrade)

action.name: emitRevenueDeltaEvent
action.description: Emit a typed revenue.mrr.delta event for the analytics-forge ingest path. Pure event payload — analytics owns persistence + bucketing.

trigger.description: charge.dispute.created or charge.dispute.updated webhook from Stripe (rare, high-stakes)

action.name: logDisputeAndNotify
action.description: INSERT INTO compliance.audit_log + UPSERT INTO compliance.disputes (the system-of-record row); also emit a typed notification event for the operator dashboard with severity heuristic (critical / warning / info).

trigger.description: Subscription cancelled — voluntary churn, involuntary (failed payment), or admin (refund/fraud/ToS). Social campaigns that re-engage churned customers (testimonial requests, "look how happy our customers are" posts, upsell drips) need to skip that contact for a cool-down window so the brand voice stays consistent with the actual relationship state.

action.name: skipAudienceSegment
action.description: Compute a skip-audience instruction for the social-forge campaign runner: contactId + skipUntil + skipSegmentTags. Tags depend on cancellation reason — voluntary (30d) skips testimonial/upsell/community; involuntary (7d) skips testimonial/spotlight only (recovery campaigns still run); admin (60d) skips everything brand-feel-good. Pure-compute helper at src/lib/bridges/payment-to-social.ts.
